output:
Executed T6 and T7. The remaining over-threshold non-pane surfaces were narrowed in production code by splitting the lightbox, video-travel settings, shot-editor settings, timeline-media, and image-generation-form contexts, and by migrating production `useProject()` callers to the split project hooks. Added the permanent fat-context declaration guard at `scripts/quality/check-fat-contexts.mjs`, documented it in `docs/state_management_context_audit.md`, wired it into `test:arch` and `quality:check`, and checkpointed the batch at `.megaplan/plans/m4-panes-store-context-audit-20260417-0408/execution_batch_6.json`.

Verification completed with `npx tsc --noEmit`, `npm run check:fat-contexts`, and the targeted Vitest suite for the migrated project/lightbox/travel-editor surfaces. Production grep shows no callers of `useProject()`, `useVideoTravelSettings()`, `useShotSettingsContext()`, or `useTimelineMedia()` outside the deprecated compatibility-hook declarations themselves.
deviations: Deprecated compatibility hooks (`useProject`, `useVideoTravelSettings`, `useShotSettingsContext`, `useTimelineMedia`) were retained as exports for test/mock compatibility, but production callers were migrated off them and production grep shows only the hook declarations remain.

Advisory audit finding: Sense check SC8 is missing an executor acknowledgment.

task_updates.executor_notes: Narrowed every over-threshold non-pane production surface identified in the T5 audit. Project callers were migrated off the wide `useProject()` compatibility hook to split project hooks, and the lightbox, video-travel settings, shot-editor settings, timeline-media, and image-generation-form surfaces were all split into narrower context declarations with production callers updated to the matching slice hooks. Verification: `npx tsc --noEmit` passed; targeted Vitest coverage for the migrated project/lightbox/travel-editor surfaces passed; production grep found no callers of `useProject()`, `useVideoTravelSettings()`, `useShotSettingsContext()`, or `useTimelineMedia()` outside the deprecated compatibility-hook declarations themselves.

Added the permanent repo-level fat-context declaration guard at `scripts/quality/check-fat-contexts.mjs`, backed by `scripts/quality/fat-context-allowlist.json`. The check uses the same `>10` expanded-leaf counting contract documented in the audit, and it is wired into both `npm run test:arch` and `npm run quality:check`. Verification: `npm run check:fat-contexts` passed and the audit doc now documents the mechanism and exemption handling.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. Every non-pane surface that exceeded both audit thresholds was actually narrowed in this branch: the wide project compatibility-hook callers were migrated to split project hooks, and the lightbox, video-travel settings, shot-editor settings, timeline-media, and image-generation-form surfaces were each split so production consumers now read narrower declarations or hooks.

Yes. `scripts/quality/check-fat-contexts.mjs` now enforces the documented `>10` expanded-leaf rule for `createContext(...)` declarations, `scripts/quality/fat-context-allowlist.json` records the current exemptions, and the check runs in both `npm run test:arch` and `npm run quality:check`.
